chunk_summary: This section discusses India's rice fortification policy, its implementation under welfare schemes, and clinic counseling to address iodine deficiency disorders, with reference to government regulations and guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of fortified rice in addressing iodine deficiency disorders in India.
Analyze the policy framework for rice fortification under national welfare schemes.
Examine clinic counseling strategies for promoting fortified rice consumption.
Review quality management protocols for fortified rice kernels (FRK).
key_facts: The Cabinet approved the continuation of free fortified rice distribution under welfare schemes from July 2024 to December 2028 (PM India, 2024).
Fortified rice kernels (FRK) must meet quality standards outlined in the SoP by DFPD (2022).
Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) issued comprehensive fortification regulations in 2021.
Fortified rice distribution aims to combat iodine deficiency disorders, a major public health concern in India.
Clinic counseling is emphasized to improve awareness and consumption of fortified rice among vulnerable populations.
reference_quote: Cabinet approves continuation of supply of free fortified rice under welfare schemes from July 2024 to December 2028 (PM India, 2024).
document_key_topics: Clinic counseling for fortified rice consumption
Quality management of fortified rice kernels
